task_completion.description: Preserve Volume '30' for 'Attention Is All You Need'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots where 'Attention Is All You Need' is being merged (in the right pane) or viewed, and the Volume field is explicitly selected or shown as '30'.
Look for screenshots where 'Deep Learning' is being merged or viewed, and the DOI field is explicitly selected or shown as '10.1038/nature14539'.

task_completion.description: Preserve Pages '379-423' for 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots where 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication' is being merged or viewed, and the Pages field is explicitly selected or shown as '379-423'.

task_completion.description: Preserve Year '1950' for 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots where 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence' is being merged or viewed, and the Date or Year field is explicitly selected or shown as '1950'.
Look for a screenshot showing the 'Duplicate Items' collection selected in the left pane of Zotero, with the middle item list pane completely empty, indicating all duplicates have been resolved.

integrity.description: The agent used the Zotero GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: The Zotero application window is visible and interacted with. There should be no terminal windows used to modify the Zotero sqlite database directly.

integrity.description: The agent actually merged the items rather than just deleting them
integrity.visual_evidence: The Zotero item merge pane (usually on the right side) is visible in the screenshots, showing the selection of metadata fields to keep from different versions of the duplicate items.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires merging 4 sets of duplicate papers in Zotero. Verify the following metadata is preserved during the merge or in the final items: 1) 'Attention Is All You Need' has Volume '30'; 2) 'Deep Learning' has DOI '10.1038/nature14539'; 3) 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication' has Pages '379-423'; 4) 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence' has Date/Year '1950'. Finally, the 'Duplicate Items' view must be empty. The library should end up with 18 unique items and 4 items in the trash.
